operating_state: Total revenue increased to $6.8 billion in Q2 2024 from $6.0 billion in Q1 2024, driven by higher domestic crude oil volumes and prices following resumed Eastern Gulf of Mexico production after third-party outage resolution in April. Oil and gas segment earnings rose 32% quarter-over-quarter with domestic oil volumes increasing 85 Mboe/d primarily from Gulf recovery. Chemical segment earnings improved 17% on higher caustic soda and PVC prices with improved product demand.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased modestly with transportation and gathering costs rising from prior quarter. Chemical segment benefited from higher product prices offsetting elevated ethylene costs. Depreciation, depletion and amortization increased to $1.8 billion from $1.7 billion, though per-unit rates declined as Gulf of Mexico production normalized.
material_events: CrownRock acquisition closed August 1, 2024 for $12.4 billion total consideration including cash, equity, and assumed debt. Company issued $9.7 billion in new debt financing comprising term loans and senior notes with maturities from 2025 to 2054. Announced Delaware Basin asset sale in July 2024 as part of $4.5-6.0 billion divestiture program. Declared $398 million in common dividends during first half 2024.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil price volatility with WTI averaging $80.56 in Q2 2024 versus $76.96 in Q1 2024. Geopolitical risks from Russia-Ukraine war and Middle East conflicts continue affecting energy markets alongside OPEC actions and U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve management. Company manages inflation impacts through operational efficiencies with substantially all debt at fixed rates.
forward_outlook: Management targets maintaining production base while delivering sustainable dividend growth through short and medium-cycle investments. Plans to complete $4.5-6.0 billion asset divestiture program within 18 months of CrownRock closing, applying proceeds to deleveraging until principal debt falls below $15 billion. Expects CrownRock acquisition to be immediately cash flow accretive while strengthening Permian Basin portfolio.
uncertainty_profile: Company faces $1.2 billion debt maturities in next 12 months with Zero Coupons puttable in October 2024. Environmental compliance costs expected to rise under stringent regulations with ongoing remediation activities at multiple sites. Credit ratings remain split with potential downgrades affecting capital access and costs.
